## PSC-HELPER v0.8.1.1 - Reliability and Security Fixes

`v0.8.1.1` is a bug-fix release for the P.OS 0.8.1 product line. It focuses on
reliable Discord action execution, persistent-state safety, moderation accuracy,
and defense in depth around AI-assisted administration.

### Highlights

- Fixed owner-issued AI administration actions so P.OS executes the verified
  Discord operation instead of printing an internal tool-call representation
- Kept direct execution for Pumba, owner approval for third-party mutations,
  protected targets, role hierarchy checks, and separate shutdown confirmation
- Removed the startup race between SQLite restore and the first backup; backups
  now use consistent gzip snapshots, integrity metadata, bounded decompression,
  SQLite validation, and atomic rollback
- Hardened prompt-injection detection, quoted-command handling, identity memory,
  output filtering, provider endpoints, and factual action-result reporting
- Expanded deterministic URL, attachment, archive, spam, raid, and media checks;
  reputation services no longer follow redirects carrying API credentials
- Added persisted Discord security-posture monitoring for MFA, verification,
  AutoMod, roles, channels, webhooks, bot permissions, and third-party admin bots
- Improved adaptive GIF encoding, timing preservation, upload-limit handling,
  processing deadlines, resource bounds, and safe user-facing errors
- Fixed duplicate form decisions, moderation/event ordering, reconnect logging,
  configuration validation, and Discord embed limit handling
- Updated the bilingual official website, documentation, CI checks, and pinned
  dependency set; `p.gif` remains the only public text command

### Upgrade notes

- No manual database migration is required
- Keep `DB_BACKUP_CHANNEL_ID` pointed at a private channel and ensure the P.OS
  role remains above roles it must manage
- Review `.env.example` before deployment; secrets and Railway settings are not
  stored in this release
- Validated with 241 tests, 36 subtests, Ruff, mypy, Bandit, pip-audit,
  dependency consistency checks, Python 3.11 compilation, and JavaScript syntax
